prompts: The urban heat island (UHI) phenomenon occurs when urban areas experience higher temperatures than their rural surroundings due to human activities. This temperature variation is primarily caused by the concentration of buildings, roads, and other infrastructure that absorb and retain heat. Vegetation, which cools areas through transpiration and provides shade, is usually sparse in urban regions compared to rural areas. Consequently, urban areas lack the natural cooling effects provided by plants, exacerbating the heat effect. In addition, waste heat generated by vehicles, factories, and air conditioning units contributes to higher urban temperatures.
